input.content:
[user]what is imitating?
[Ninereeds]Imitating is copying actions of another living thing.
Imitating is matching sounds made by others nearby.
Imitating is mirroring movements seen in the world.
Imitating is repeating gestures from companions.
Imitating is following patterns shown by teachers.
Imitating is observation and reproduction.

[user]when does imitating happen?
[Ninereeds]Imitating happens when a young one watches an older one.
Imitating happens when a student sees a teacher demonstrate.
Imitating happens during play with other children.
Imitating happens in moments of focused attention.
Imitating happens during daily routines and rituals.
Imitating happens with full attention and careful observation.

[user]what does imitating bring?
[Ninereeds]Imitating brings understanding of new skills.
Imitating brings connection between teacher and student.
Imitating brings confidence through successful copying.
Imitating brings mastery of useful techniques.
Imitating brings belonging within a group.
Imitating brings skill development and social bonding.

[user]what is imitating for?
[Ninereeds]Imitating is for learning how to perform new actions.
Imitating is for absorbing cultural practices.
Imitating is for building communication abilities.
Imitating is for developing physical coordination.
Imitating is for understanding social roles.
Imitating is for acquiring skills and building bonds.
